advantages: Code is composable — pipelines and notebooks reuse the same logic.
Cheap imports keep test/CI startup fast.
Clear public/private boundary lets internals evolve safely.
Dependency injection makes both production and tests cleaner.
Modules become long-lived assets; one well-shaped utility module is used for years.
disadvantages: More upfront design than a script (think about API surface, naming, types).
Over-engineering risk — splitting code into modules before there's a second caller.
Wider blast radius for breaking changes once a module is depended on broadly.
Discipline required to keep import-time work near zero as the module grows.
Versioning becomes a thing once external callers exist.
